    Thag 3:5  Mātaṅgaputta
            
              It’s too cold,
              too hot,
              too late in the evening—
              people who say this,
              shirking their work:
              The moment passes them by.
              
                Whoever regards cold & heat
                as no more than grass,
                doing his manly duties,
                won’t fall away
                from ease.
              
              
                With my chest
                I push through wild grasses—
                spear-grass,
                ribbon-grass,
                rushes—
                cultivating a heart
                bent on seclusion.
              
            
            See also: AN 8:95
description:
> doing his manly duties,  
[he] won’t fall away  
from ease.

A short poem on arousing energy in the practice.
